response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack web engineer with strong TypeScript/Node.js and DLang experience, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its core requirement. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. There is no evidence of industrial networking protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle practices, multi-threaded systems programming, or industrial/IoT domain knowledge — all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Broad exposure to event-driven and distributed architectures (GISCollective, Sociomantic high-throughput microservices) aligns partially with message-broker requirements.
Demonstrated agile practices, TDD discipline, mentoring, and community knowledge-sharing align with the role's behavioral competencies.
Experience with REST APIs, Node.js, and React covers several of the advantageous/secondary skill requirements.
concerns: C++ proficiency is minimal and dated (≤6 months of production use in 2014–2015); the entire primary stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang, not C++.
No evidence of industrial cybersecurity knowledge, IEC 62443 compliance, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices.
No exposure to industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or the industrial/OT domain the role operates in.
